name: feat-ESE-19
title: GPU 2D physics: circles and world boundaries
description:
Implement a 2D GPU physics system alongside the existing 3D system. Entities must be assignable to either 2D or 3D physics processing — not both.

Collider types for 2D:
- **GpuCircleCollider**: radius-based, equivalent to sphere in 3D
- **GpuWorldBoundary**: infinite line defined by normal (nx, ny) and distance from origin. Entities collide against the normal side. Godot calls this WorldBoundaryShape2D.

Architecture must define:
- How 2D vs 3D physics selection works (component composition: GpuBody2D vs GpuBody3D tag?)
- Whether 2D and 3D share the spatial hash grid or use separate grids
- How 2D entities interact with the existing Transform component (only px, py used; pz ignored)
- World boundary representation: static entities with boundary collider, or a separate uniform/buffer?
- Integration with the existing kernel DSL and field-level binding selection

Start with architecture document, then implementation subtasks.
acceptanceCriteria:
- Architecture document covering 2D/3D selection, collider types, grid strategy, and Transform usage
- GpuBody2D tag component distinguishes 2D from 3D physics entities
- GpuCircleCollider component (radius field)
- GpuWorldBoundary component (nx, ny, distance fields) for infinite collision planes
- 2D spatial hash grid (separate from 3D)
- 2D narrowphase: circle-circle and circle-boundary collision + impulse response
- 2D integration kernel with gravity and boundary bounce
- Demo: circles bouncing inside world boundaries, auto-scaling to 30fps
- All within WebGPU default storage buffer limits using field-level binding selection
demoDeliverable: Storybook demo story: circles falling under 2D gravity, bouncing off each other and world boundaries. Auto-scaling body count until 30fps. World boundaries visible as lines. HUD shows body count and fps.
testingScenarios:
- 2D bodies use only px, py from Transform (pz unchanged)
- Circle-circle collision detected and resolved correctly
- Circle-boundary collision bounces off normal side
- 2D and 3D bodies coexist in the same world without interference
- Storage buffer count within default limits
- Performance: auto-scales to find 30fps threshold
